title: Deuteronomy: Covenant Commands
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of deuteronomy: covenant commands and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study deuteronomy: covenant commands and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.
Review question: What is the Shema, and where is it found? Answer: Hear O Israel: the LORD our God, the LORD is one. It is found in Deuteronomy 6:4.
segments.correct_answer: Hear O Israel: the LORD our God, the LORD is one — Deuteronomy 6:4
acceptable_variants: Shema Yisrael Adonai Eloheinu Adonai Echad — Deuteronomy 6:4
the LORD is one — in Deuteronomy chapter 6
the jewish confession of God's unity in Deuteronomy 6

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: Hebrew for hear; the opening word of Deuteronomy 6:4 and the title for Israel's central confession of faith in the oneness of God, recited morning and evening
The great commandment of Deuteronomy 6:5 — to love God with all heart, soul, and strength; the entire law flows from and returns to this total devotion
